consensus_findings.description: read_manifest() and read_vendor_findings() do not validate manifest schema_version. The contract (review-cache-layout.schema.json) explicitly mandates readers MUST refuse unknown versions; the implementation silently accepts any version and would misinterpret a future v2 manifest as v1.

convergence_loop.py:264 writes checkpoints to <artifacts_dir>/reviews/round-{N}/ but the spec, design, and proposal all specify <artifacts_dir>/.review-cache/. Operators following documented manual-recovery procedure look in the wrong place.

consensus_findings.verification: confirmed against review_dispatcher.py:1180-1216 — docstring acknowledges silent loss
consensus_findings.description: ReviewOrchestrator.write_manifest() backward-compat shim accepts output_path but ignores output_path.name (always writes review-manifest.json under output_path.parent). Caller passing a custom filename gets silent loss with no error.

consensus_findings.verification: confirmed against checkpoint_findings.py:257-261 — only vendors[].name is path-safety-checked
consensus_findings.description: write_manifest() validates only vendors[].name (path-safety regex), not vendors[].findings_path or finding_count. A buggy or malicious caller can write a manifest with traversal-laden findings_path that the reader later refuses, producing a manifest-on-disk-but-unreadable failure mode.

consensus_findings.verification: confirmed against convergence_loop.py:288-300 + checkpoint_findings.py:268-269 — vendors_index only includes successful reviews
consensus_findings.description: convergence_loop.py:293-300 calls cf_write_manifest without quorum_requested. Default is len(vendors), but vendors_index only contains successful reviews (line 278 condition), so a 3-of-3 attempt with 2 successes records quorum_requested=2 instead of 3, misrepresenting the round's intent.

consensus_findings.verification: confirmed against checkpoint_findings.py:73-79 — os.fsync(dirfd) is POSIX-only
consensus_findings.description: _atomic_write_json calls os.fsync on the parent directory file descriptor. This is POSIX-specific (Windows lacks dir fds) and on some macOS filesystems may raise OSError(EINVAL). Wrap in try/except OSError so a failed dir-fsync does not crash the write — the file fsync at line 72 is the load-bearing durability call.

consensus_findings.verification: confirmed by code reading — no try/except around the open block
consensus_findings.description: _atomic_write_json leaves <path>.tmp behind when json.dump/flush/fsync raises mid-write. Atomic-rename invariant is preserved (no partial target file), but residue accumulates under chronic disk pressure. Wrap in try/except that calls tmp.unlink(missing_ok=True) on non-replace failure, then re-raises.

summary.convergence: round-1-blocking — applying targeted fixes (5 commits) before round 2 decision
summary.vendor_diversity_notes: Each vendor caught issues the others missed: codex flagged spec/code drift + write-shim quirks (which require deep knowledge of the dispatcher backward-compat); gemini caught accounting + cross-platform durability; claude_code spotted disk-hygiene leak.
